title: Chat REPL that answers — walking skeleton, chat role, resumable session
description: Build the thin `bober chat` REPL skeleton: a ChatSession turn loop that reads the disk roster and the .bober/memory/ distill, classifies each turn with one loose-JSON LLMClient.chat call (jsonObjectMode), and answers. Add a dedicated `chat` role to BoberConfig (Zod + defaults) and resolve its provider via resolveRoleProviders/createClient so it runs identically on Anthropic and DeepSeek. Persist conversation to a resumable per-session JSONL and wire deterministic slash-commands /runs, /exit, /help. Spawn and steer-stop are NOT implemented this sprint — the classifier may emit spawn/steer but the session replies that those actions arrive in a later sprint (graceful stub).

successCriteria.description: The project builds successfully with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript compilation produces zero type errors (strict mode).
All tests pass with zero failures, including new collocated tests for this sprint (npm run test).

BoberConfig parses a `chat` role (Zod schema in src/config/schema.ts) with model and provider fields; defaults (src/config/defaults.ts) set it to model opus, provider anthropic. A unit test parses a config with a DeepSeek chat role override successfully.

TurnClassifier.classify calls LLMClient.chat with jsonObjectMode:true and returns a ClassifierAction; given malformed/non-JSON text it returns {action:"answer"} (covered by a unit test with a fake LLMClient).

RosterReader.read delegates to readRunStatesFromDisk (NOT RunManager.load); a unit test writes a 'running' state.json to a temp .bober/runs and asserts RosterReader still reports it as 'running' after read. Grep confirms src/chat contains no reference to RunManager.load.

ConversationStore appends turns to .bober/chat/<sessionId>.jsonl and loadRecent returns them newest-last; a unit test creates a store, appends, then a NEW store instance with the same sessionId loads the prior turns (resume).
No @anthropic-ai/sdk or openai SDK import appears anywhere under src/chat (grep returns nothing); all LLM interaction goes through src/providers.

Deterministic slash-commands /help, /runs, and /exit are handled WITHOUT an LLM call: /runs prints the roster summary, /help lists commands, /exit ends the loop. A unit test verifies the slash dispatcher routes these without invoking the injected LLMClient.

nonGoals: Do not implement the spawn action handler (RunSpawner) — that is Sprint 2
Do not implement completion weaving / history tailing — that is Sprint 3
Do not implement steer-stop / kill-by-PID — that is Sprint 4
Do not modify runPipeline, RunManager, EventStreamManager, memory, or provider public APIs
Do not add the --run-id flag yet (Sprint 2)
stopConditions: Running `bober chat` (or the session.handleTurn entry) answers a plain question using roster + memory distill context and resumes prior conversation on a second launch with the same session id
definitionOfDone: A new src/chat/ module exposes a ChatSession with start() and handleTurn(input). Each turn reads the disk roster (RosterReader -> readRunStatesFromDisk), reads the .bober/memory/ distill, and classifies via TurnClassifier (one jsonObjectMode LLMClient.chat call, parse-fail -> answer). The answer path composes roster + distill + recent history into one Answerer LLMClient.chat call. A `chat` role exists in BoberConfig with opus/anthropic defaults, overridable to DeepSeek, and the session builds its client via resolveRoleProviders + createClient. Conversation persists to a resumable .bober/chat/<sessionId>.jsonl. Slash-commands /runs, /help, /exit work deterministically. spawn/steer classifier outputs are acknowledged as not-yet-available. Build, typecheck, and all tests pass; no SDK types leak into src/chat.
assumptions: A stable session id (e.g. the literal 'default' per project) is acceptable for resume in Phase 1; multi-session handshakes are deferred
The memory distill can be read as a plain string for prompt context via the existing reader (src/state/memory.ts:196,242,272)
The `bober chat <team>` team argument is optional in Phase 1 and ignored beyond labeling (fixed programming role set)
outOfScope: Any change to existing public APIs
generatorNotes:
Create src/chat/ with one small file per component (principles: small single-purpose modules, section comments with unicode box headers).

Verified reuse signatures:
- readRunStatesFromDisk(projectRoot): Promise<RunState[]> — src/state/run-state.ts:110. NEVER call RunManager.load() (it reconciles running->failed, run-manager.ts:251-256).
- RunState shape — src/mcp/run-manager.ts:35-55.
- LLMClient.chat(params): Promise<ChatResponse> — src/providers/types.ts:216. ChatParams has jsonObjectMode?:boolean at :183 (use true for the classifier). ChatResponse.text is the reply (:194-206). Message/TextMessage at :96-132 (role 'user'|'assistant', content).
- createClient(provider?, endpoint?, providerConfig?, model?, role?): LLMClient — src/providers/factory.ts:172. resolveRoleProviders(config): RoleProviderMap — src/config/role-providers.ts:92. Add 'chat' to the RoleName/RoleProviderMap surface and to BoberConfig schema + defaults.
- Memory distill reader — src/state/memory.ts:196 (appendLesson), :242 (loadLessonIndex), :272 (loadLesson). Compose a compact string for the prompt.

Classifier: system prompt instructs the model to return ONLY a JSON object {"action":"answer"|"spawn"|"steer", ...}. Request jsonObjectMode:true. Parse defensively: strip code fences, extract first balanced {...}, zod-validate against the ClassifierAction schema; ANY failure => {action:"answer"}. Keep the ClassifierAction type exactly as in the architecture (answer | spawn{task} | steer{op:inspect} | steer{op:stop,runId}) even though only answer is handled this sprint.

ConversationStore: append-only JSONL at .bober/chat/<sessionId>.jsonl using node:fs/promises (async only). loadRecent(limit) reads + parses, skips malformed lines, returns newest-last. Use ensureDir from src/utils/fs.ts (or src/state/helpers.ts).

Slash-command dispatcher: if input.trimStart().startsWith('/'), handle deterministically and return WITHOUT touching the LLMClient. /runs => RosterReader.summarize(await RosterReader.read()). /help => static text. /exit => signal loop end.

Wire a `bober chat` CLI command (src/cli/commands/) that constructs the ChatSession with a real client from config and calls start(). Register it in the CLI entry (mirror how `run` is registered).

Tests: collocate *.test.ts. Use a fake LLMClient (returns canned ChatResponse) for classifier + answerer tests — do NOT hit a network. Use temp dirs for ConversationStore + RosterReader (principle: no fs mocks, real temp dirs, clean up).
evaluatorNotes:
sc-1-4: parse a config object with chat:{model:'deepseek-chat',provider:'deepseek'} and assert success; assert defaults give opus/anthropic.
sc-1-5: call classify with a fake LLMClient returning '```json {\"action\":\"answer\"}```', then returning garbage 'not json' — both must yield a valid action, garbage => answer.
sc-1-6: write a temp .bober/runs/<id>/state.json with status 'running'; after RosterReader.read() the same status must still be 'running' on disk AND in the returned array. Grep src/chat for 'RunManager' — load() must not appear.
sc-1-7: append two turns, construct a fresh ConversationStore with the same sessionId+projectRoot, loadRecent(10) returns the two turns in order.
sc-1-8: grep -r '@anthropic-ai/sdk\|from "openai"' src/chat — expect no matches.
sc-1-9: feed '/runs','/help','/exit' to a session whose injected LLMClient throws if called; the turn must succeed without calling it.
Run npm run build, npm run typecheck (tsc), npm run test.
